name: Listmonk Newsletter & Mailing List API
what_it_does: Open-source self-hosted newsletter and mailing list manager with REST API — providing subscriber management, campaign creation, email sending via external SMTP/API, template management, and analytics without SaaS costs.
use_cases: Managing newsletter subscribers and segmented lists programmatically via API
Creating and scheduling email campaigns from automation agents
Importing and syncing subscriber lists from other systems via REST API
Querying campaign delivery statistics and open rates for reporting agents
Building custom subscriber onboarding flows that trigger via API
not_for: Cloud-hosted email marketing without self-hosting infrastructure
Advanced marketing automation with behavioral triggers (use Mautic)
Transactional email (listmonk is for bulk newsletters, not individual triggers)
best_when: A developer or team needs privacy-first, self-hosted newsletter management with no per-subscriber fees and full API control.
avoid_when: You need a managed SaaS solution without self-hosting infrastructure.

auth.notes: HTTP Basic auth with admin username and password. No token-based auth — basic credentials for all API calls. Self-hosted — credentials configured in install. API tokens available in newer versions.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Completely free and open source (AGPL v3); no limits on subscribers or campaigns
pricing.paid_tiers: No paid tier — open source only
pricing.notes: AGPL v3 open source — completely free. Only cost is infrastructure (VPS, SMTP service). Docker image available. Can handle millions of subscribers on modest hardware.

performance.rate_limits: No API rate limits — self-hosted only

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API documented in listmonk docs. Simple and consistent API design. Error responses are JSON. No webhooks — polling required for campaign status. Local API has low latency.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Subscriber creation idempotent by email. Campaign creation creates new campaigns. List assignments are idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Basic auth sends credentials with every request — use HTTPS only
No webhooks — agents must poll for campaign send completion
SMTP configuration required separately — listmonk doesn't include email delivery
Self-hosted setup required — Docker or binary installation with PostgreSQL

security_score_components.security_notes: AGPL v3 open source — full audit possible. Basic auth (credentials sent each request) is less secure than token auth — use HTTPS. No API rate limits (DoS risk without reverse proxy). Self-hosted means full data control.
